a: A publisher for the authorization status property.
The center for requesting authorization to provide parental controls.
The status of your app’s authorization to provide parental controls.
The person, parent, or guardian approved the request for authorization.
The person, parent, or guardian approved the request for authorization with access to non-tokenized family activity data.
The person, parent, or guardian denied the request for authorization.
The app hasn’t requested authorization.
Provide users with a read-only, visual representation of an application, category, or web domain.
Authorize your app to provide parental controls on a device.
An interface to a person’s family activity data.
A type-erased view representing the icon of the family activity.
A view in which users specify applications, web domains, and categories without revealing their choices to the app.
A collection of applications, categories, and web domains selected by the user.
A type-erased view representing the title of the family activity.
Errors the Family Controls framework reports.
The device must have a passcode set in order for an individual to enroll with parental controls.
The parent or guardian canceled a request for authorization.
Another authorized app already provides parental controls.
The device isn’t signed into a valid iCloud account.
The method’s arguments are invalid.
The device must be connected to the network in order to enroll with parental controls.
A restriction prevents your app from using Family Controls on this device.
The calling app must be authorized to provide parental controls and access data.
The system failed to set up the Family Control framework.
The type of account that Family Controls is currently managing.
A value indicating that Family Controls is managing a child account, so that a parent or guardian must enter their authorization credentials.
A value indicating that Family Controls is managing an individual account, so that the user can enter their authorization credentials.
Register your app and its Screen Time API app extensions to use Family Controls.
Apply standard modifiers to configure the family activity picker view and the views it contains.
The set of all possible activity categories.
Tokens that represent applications selected by the user.
A set of application instances selected by the user.
The status of your app’s authorization to provide parental controls.
The content of this view.
A set of category instances selected by the user.
Tokens that represent categories selected by the user.
A nonlocalized description of the authorization value, suitable for debugging.
A nonlocalized description of the type of account, suitable for debugging.
A nonlocalized description of the error, suitable for debugging.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the selection should include applications and web domains from the selected categories.
Creates a new activity selection instance.
Creates a new activity picker with optional header and footer text.
Creates a new activity selection instance.
Creates a new activity picker.
Applications someone installs on a device.
Requests authorization to provide parental controls for a child.
Requests authorization to provide parental controls for a child or individual.
Revokes authorization to provide parental controls.
A shared instance for accessing a person’s family activity data.
The shared center for requesting and revoking authorization.
Web domains someone visits on their device.
Tokens that represent web domains selected by the user.
A set of web domain instances selected by the user.
